sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.37 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: Is it true that for every finite group$G$and every factorization$|G|=ab$there exist subsets$A,B G$with$|A|=a$and$|B|=b$such that$G=AB$? Cf. 19.35 in Archive. Comment of 2025: any minimal counterexample$G$must be a simple group without prime-index subgroups (R.\,R.\,Bildanov, V.\,A.\,Goryachenko, A.\,V.\,Vasil'ev, Siberian Electron. Math. Rep., 17 (2020), 683--689; \ M.\,H.\,Hooshmand, , 49, no.\,7...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
